spec_constraints_from_council: 2.1 exists-when MUST require >=3-directive batch failing at NON-FIRST index with exact verb+path+non-zero boundary toEqual — single-directive tests cannot see the instructions[0] bug (BA halt-worthy)
reason enum MUST discriminate the known rejection families — a one-value closed enum passes the letter and delivers nothing (BA)

Every reason/origin VALUE gated by tech-writer: author vocabulary only — reject serialization/round-trip/protocol/directive/batch/emit; origin values in author mental-model terms, not 'SDK-origin' jargon (BA)

Applied boundary expressed in author-action terms OR explicitly scoped 'directive-level 1:1 with verbs in v1, revisited at Stage 5' — coalescing (ADR-0006) will silently misreport otherwise (BA freeze-now defect)
Flush-time vs commit-time: give an author-actionable discriminant or fold into origin/reason — don't freeze a distinction the author can't perceive (BA)
Add one e2e author-reads-the-error scenario (zero e2e rejection coverage exists) (BA)
FIT-11 leak scan = THE invalidating absence: all families incl. the 3 RAW-UNTIL-STAGE-2.1, dictionary seeded from the fake's actual strings, red-proof fixture mandatory (PM+QA)

2.4 constrained: origin discriminant lands in the frozen type; proof breadth = ONE concrete case (currentContext misuse vs engine rejection); dialect-family proof deferred to Stage 5 ADR-0012 (PM tripwire)
context.ts double-fault row = DOCUMENT-ONLY, re-register as still-open; any design touching E1/E2 handling is out of scope — halt (PM tripwire)
Literal-message pins updated in the SAME slice that changes the format (PM sequencing)
Type freezes FIRST (reason enum + origin discriminant + boundary field + verb fix = ONE type-shape decision) before any attribution wiring (PM sequencing)
Enum growth semantics vs exhaustive author switches: semver stance recorded in the D2 ADR
2.3 formally droppable slice, never entangled with 2.1 slices; helper + case names author-friendly (absent/empty/present)
priority_rank: 2.1 > 2.2 (fused with 2.1 — one contract) > 2.4-minimum (discriminant in type) > 2.3 (severable); ONE invalidating absence = FIT-11 all-family leak scan; runner-up = origin discriminant missing from frozen type (Stage 5 would reopen the contract)
d2_ratified: 2026-07-05 (owner): OPTION (b) — closed SDK reason enum. AuthoringError.reason drawn from a closed SDK-owned vocabulary (collision | not-found | cap-exceeded | serialization | ...exact set fixed at spec). NO engine string anywhere in the error object — no-engine-text guarantee (REQ-11/12.2/13.1) held by construction; FIT-11 whole-object scan stays as safety net. Formalize as ADR at design.
api_promotion_ratified: 2026-07-05 (owner): AuthoringError (+ field types) promoted to PUBLIC via ./commons re-export (single author surface, no new subpath). Verb vocabulary corrected to AUTHOR verbs — verb: 'remove', never wire 'delete' (resolves ADR-0013 conflict). ADR at design documents the ADR-0009 boundary crossing + semver-frozen field set. FIT-04 baseline regen deliberate.

Out: Stages 3/4/5/6, real engine.

open_questions_product: D2 structured-cause shape: option space (a) no cause / (b) closed SDK reason enum [architect-recommended] / (c) structured SDK-vocabulary cause object / (d) raw engine text [violates no-engine-text guarantee, needs explicit owner trade]

AuthoringError public promotion: verb vocabulary correction (wire 'delete' → author 'remove', ADR-0013 conflict) + which public subpath re-exports the type (./commons vs dedicated) — crosses ADR-0009 boundary, needs ADR
open_questions_technical: Mechanism for failedIndex + applied-boundary metadata on emit rejection (3 approaches evaluated by explore; #2 recommended: extend rejection error object, not port signature)
2.4 origin-taxonomy mechanism given SDK-origin dialect family doesn't exist yet (reserve forward-compat slot without implementing)
explore_findings_key: session.ts:62 instructions[0] hardcode has ZERO red-proof (no multi-directive mid-chain test exists — REQ-11 test is single-directive, cannot distinguish fix from hack); AuthoringError/AuthoringVerb NOT exported publicly (promotion = new API decision, ADR-0009 crossing); AuthoringError.verb leaks wire vocabulary ('delete' not 'remove', contradicts ADR-0013); applied boundary exists only transiently in fake emit loop — discard() erases it, MUST travel in structured rejection; 11 rejection sites: 3 batch-level (B1 serialization, B2 cap — NO offending verb, B3 round-trip) vs 8 directive-level (D1-D8) — different attribution contracts; multi-flush cross-batch boundary undecided (cumulative run-level vs per-batch); commit-time rejection UNREACHABLE with fake (theatre risk — spec must declare non-site or wrap+test); remove/delete never rejects (intentional non-site); existing leak scan weak (message+stack only, dead OpMove assertion) — replace not extend; context.ts frozen-E1 + non-Error-E1 branches untested (mutation leaks); batch-cap.test.ts:67 literal-message pins break under structured rejection unless carried forward
d2_option_space: (a) no cause — rejected by problem statement; (b) closed SDK reason enum (collision|not-found|cap-exceeded|serialization|conflict...) — no engine string anywhere, guarantee held by construction [architect default]; (c) structured cause object SDK-vocabulary fields (richer, e.g. collidingPath; scan must recurse cause); (d) raw engine text as cause — violates REQ-11/12.2/13.1, only if owner explicitly trades the guarantee
design_risks_forward: D2 vs no-engine-text guarantee: likeliest subtle regression site
Batch-level (cap) attribution must NOT be forced onto a verb — aggregate contract
Applied boundary must span prior successful flushes in the run OR be explicitly per-batch (decide at spec)
SDK-origin errors born at specific throw sites (currentContext), NEVER blanket-wrapped — E1 may be author's own throw (double-fault test pins toBe(e1))
currentContext() has no verb/path — AuthoringError needs origin-tagged discriminated shape or optional fields
FIT-11 = NEW fitness (runtime value-content deep-walk + red-proof), not FIT-10 extension; FIT-10 stays structural guard
FIT-04 baseline regen deliberate, additivity argued out-of-band
context.test.ts currentContext plain-Error pin legitimately breaks under 2.4 — plan it, don't loosen it
Non-Error rejection values: structured-cause read must degrade gracefully (throw 'string'/42/undefined)

spec_notable: Frozen vocabulary: reason = path-collision|path-not-found|unrepresentable-content|changes-too-large|outside-run|unknown; origin = write-rejected|authoring-rejected; helper = classifyContent + exported ContentState union. EmitRejection {code, failedIndex?, appliedCount} port-internal, code closed set (collision|not-found|unrepresentable|cap) set at fake throw sites, message-text classification BANNED (decoy scenario ERM-01.3). Message contract = REQ-AEC-06 3-row table (never interpolates undefined). Enum growth = MAJOR semver. appliedCount = per-batch diagnostic, JSDoc anti-comprehension-trap obligated. 5 non-sites: commit-time, remove, read-transport, double-fault, template-render. Design must name: message embeds reason value (rename breaks both pins — intentional); code set coarse by design (4 codes), Stage-6 port-convention revisit noted in ADR.
